event: Villa Medici in Rome (Facade of the Grotto Logia)
symbols: arched gateway — threshold and passage, evoking the biblical gate motif of entry into consecrated space
cypress trees — mourning, eternity, and the boundary between life and death in Mediterranean tradition
enclosed garden with high walls — echoes of the hortus conclusus, the enclosed garden of Song of Solomon 4:12
twilight sky — the liminal hour between day and night, evoking contemplation of passing time
balustrade and upper terrace — elevation and separation between earthly and higher realms
sculptural figure above the arch — presiding presence watching over the threshold
description: A villa facade with a large arched gateway at center, flanked by weathered walls. Above the arch, a balustrade runs across the upper level with a small sculptural figure at center. Behind the structure, tall dark cypress trees rise dramatically against a dusky grey-green sky, towering over the architecture. The gateway is closed with a wooden lattice gate. The palette is dark, dominated by deep greens, browns, and muted earth tones, bathed in dim light suggesting twilight.

_meta.title: Villa Medici in Rome (Facade of the Grotto Logia)
